data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-10T00:53:59.979Z</current_datetime>

I have an EF Core 8 app. These two operations are extremely slow because they load all entities into memory:

```csharp
// Operation 1: Deactivate all products not sold in 6 months
var staleProducts = await _db.Products
    .Where(p => p.LastSoldDate < DateTime.UtcNow.AddMonths(-6))
    .ToListAsync();
foreach (var p in staleProducts)
{
    p.IsActive = false;
}
await _db.SaveChangesAsync();

// Operation 2: Delete all audit logs older than 1 year
var oldLogs = await _db.AuditLogs
    .Where(l => l.CreatedAt < DateTime.UtcNow.AddYears(-1))
    .ToListAsync();
_db.AuditLogs.RemoveRange(oldLogs);
await _db.SaveChangesAsync();
```

There are 500K products and 2M audit logs. How do I make these efficient?

data.content:
Use EF Core 7+'s **`ExecuteUpdateAsync`** and **`ExecuteDeleteAsync`** â€” they translate directly to SQL, bypassing entity tracking entirely:

```csharp
// Operation 1: bulk UPDATE in a single SQL statement
await _db.Products
    .Where(p => p.LastSoldDate < DateTime.UtcNow.AddMonths(-6))
    .ExecuteUpdateAsync(s => s.SetProperty(p => p.IsActive, false));

// Operation 2: bulk DELETE in a single SQL statement
await _db.AuditLogs
    .Where(l => l.CreatedAt < DateTime.UtcNow.AddYears(-1))
    .ExecuteDeleteAsync();
```

**Why this is faster:**

| | Old approach | `ExecuteUpdate/Delete` |
|---|---|---|
| SQL roundtrips | SELECT + N UPDATEs/DELETEs | 1 single statement |
| Memory | loads all entities | zero entities loaded |
| Change tracker | tracks every entity | bypassed entirely |

**Caveats:**
- These execute **immediately** (no `SaveChangesAsync` needed).
- They **bypass the change tracker**, so any already-loaded entities won't reflect the change â€” call `_db.ChangeTracker.Clear()` afterward if needed.
- For the 2M-row delete, consider batching if your DB has lock-escalation concerns:

```csharp
int deleted;
do
{
    deleted = await _db.AuditLogs
        .Where(l => l.CreatedAt < DateTime.UtcNow.AddYears(-1))
        .Take(10_000)
        .ExecuteDeleteAsync();
} while (deleted > 0);
```
data.reasoningText: The user is asking about EF Core 8 bulk operations. EF Core 7+ introduced `ExecuteUpdateAsync` and `ExecuteDeleteAsync` which translate directly to SQL `UPDATE` and `DELETE` statements without loading entities into memory.
